question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open a large number of simultaneous TCP connections to the gRPC port (5600) and on each connection saturate all 5 slots enforced by maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection(), thereby exhausting the applicationTaskExecutor thread pool and preventing legitimate transaction-gossip subscription calls from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-IP connection limit in grpcServerConfigurer() by opening thousands of TCP connections from a single IP, each consuming maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() slots, causing a full denial-of-service that silences gossip delivery to all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user initiate exactly maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() long-lived streaming RPC calls per connection and then stall them indefinitely (slow-read attack), blocking the applicationTaskExecutor from dispatching new gossip-related calls on those connections and causing transaction delivery to halt?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor assigned via serverBuilder.executor() by flooding the gRPC server with concurrent streaming subscriptions across many connections, starving the executor of threads and preventing gossip messages from being forwarded to legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() limit by repeatedly opening and closing connections in rapid succession, creating a connection churn that exhausts OS-level socket resources and prevents the gRPC server from accepting new connections needed for gossip propagation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send malformed or oversized gRPC frames on a connection to trigger error-handling paths in the NettyServerBuilder pipeline, causing the applicationTaskExecutor to be consumed by error processing and delaying or dropping gossip transaction messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a maxConnectionAge or maxConnectionIdle setting in grpcServerConfigurer() to hold connections open indefinitely, accumulating stale connections that consume executor threads and prevent new gossip-related subscriptions from being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxConnectionsTotal cap in grpcServerConfigurer() to open an unbounded number of connections, each holding maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() active streams, until the JVM heap or file descriptor limit is exhausted and gossip delivery is completely halted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a continuous stream of PING frames or HTTP/2 SETTINGS frames to the NettyServerBuilder-backed server, forcing the applicationTaskExecutor to process control frames and starving gossip message dispatch threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a flow-control window limit to send large gRPC request payloads that back-pressure the server's Netty pipeline, causing gossip subscription responses to be queued indefinitely and effectively suppressing transaction delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor by submitting CPU-intensive or blocking gRPC calls that monopolize executor threads, preventing the server from processing gossip-related streaming RPCs for other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a keepAliveTimeout in grpcServerConfigurer() to keep half-open TCP connections alive without sending data, consuming Netty channel resources and preventing the server from accepting new gossip subscription connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() value of 5 (from NettyProperties) by opening connections from multiple source IPs (e.g., via a botnet or spoofed addresses) to multiply the total number of blocked call slots and prevent any gossip transactions from reaching legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC request with an extremely large metadata header to the NettyServerBuilder-backed server, causing memory pressure in the applicationTaskExecutor's thread pool and delaying gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxInboundMessageSize restriction in grpcServerConfigurer() to send arbitrarily large gRPC request bodies, causing heap exhaustion in the JVM and crashing the server, thereby preventing all gossip transaction delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxInboundMetadataSize restriction in grpcServerConfigurer() to send requests with enormous metadata, causing the Netty pipeline to allocate excessive memory and starving gossip message processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not set a handshakeTimeout on the NettyServerBuilder to initiate TLS handshakes and then stall them indefinitely, consuming Netty worker threads and preventing new gossip subscription connections from being established?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting in grpcServerConfigurer() to send thousands of rapid-fire gRPC unary calls per second, overwhelming the applicationTaskExecutor queue and causing gossip streaming subscriptions to time out or be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor (set via serverBuilder.executor()) by opening many connections and issuing calls that trigger expensive database reads via the readOnly TransactionTemplate, starving the executor and blocking gossip message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-connection bandwidth throttle in grpcServerConfigurer() to send data at maximum line rate, saturating the server's network interface and preventing gossip transaction messages from being transmitted to legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() is enforced per-connection but not globally, by distributing calls across many connections to exceed any implicit global concurrency limit and prevent gossip-related calls from being scheduled?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a deadline or timeout enforcement in grpcServerConfigurer() to initiate streaming calls that never complete, holding applicationTaskExecutor threads indefinitely and preventing gossip transaction messages from being processed for other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Netty HTTP/2 WINDOW_UPDATE mechanism by sending WINDOW_UPDATE frames with zero increment to stall the server's outbound flow control, preventing gossip transaction messages from being written to the wire for all subscribers on that connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxRstFramesPerWindow or RST_STREAM rate limit in grpcServerConfigurer() to send rapid RST_STREAM frames, causing the server to repeatedly tear down and re-establish streams and preventing stable gossip subscription delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a GOAWAY frame rate limit in grpcServerConfigurer() to force the server into repeated connection teardown cycles, disrupting gossip transaction streaming for all active subscribers?
